answer: Vietnam's official population estimate for 2025 is around 100 million. The UN projected a total of 101.6 million. The population growth rate was expected to be minimal.

results.title: Vietnam Population (1950-2025) - Macrotrends
results.snippet: ## Vietnam Population (1950-2025). Population Economy Trade Health Education Development Labor Force Environment Crime Immigration Other. Population Growth Rate Density Urban Rural Life Expectancy Birth Rate Death Rate Infant Mortality Rate Fertility Rate. ##### Total current population for Vietnam in 2025 was **100,103,979**, a **0.61% increase** from 2024. * Total population for Vietnam in 2024 was **99,497,680**, a **0.85% decline** from 2023. * Total population for Vietnam in 2023 was **100,352,192**, a **0.67% increase** from 2022. * Total population for Vietnam in 2022 was **99,680,655**, a **0.75% increase** from 2021. Total population is based on the de facto definition of population, which counts all residents regardless of legal status or citizenship. The values shown are midyear estimates. The full historical dataset is available for download here: Population of Vietnam | Historical Data | 1950 - 2025.

results.title: Viet Nam Population 2025
results.snippet: Total population in millions, 2025: 101.6. Population annual doubling time, years, 2025: 118. Population aged 0-14, per cent, 2025: 23.

results.title: Demographics of Vietnam
results.snippet: Demographics of Vietnam ; 102,300,000 (December 2025) · 310/km² · 0.97% (2021 est.) · 15.2 births/1000 population (2022 est.)

results.title: Vietnam Population Pyramid 2025 - Demographics & Birth Statistics
results.snippet: **📅 Next Update:** Vietnam fertility rate 2026 data will be released with UN World Population Prospects 2026 revision.Current projections suggest continued decline in birth rates,impacting long-term demographic planning and economic policies. Vietnam is experiencing an optimal demographic dividend window, with a favorable dependency ratio of 47.8 and 67.6% working-age population. Vietnam experiences rapid population aging at 4.3 years median age increase per decade, indicating compressed demographic transition typical of developing economies. Comprehensive charts showing Vietnam's demographic trends, age structure evolution, and current population distribution patterns. **Current Demographic Balance:** This distribution shows Vietnam's present age structure composition, highlighting the relative size of dependent populations (youth and elderly) versus the productive working-age group. These visualizations collectively tell the story of Vietnam's demographic evolution, revealing patterns in population growth, aging trends, and structural changes that shape current social and economic realities. At 34.4 years, Vietnam shows a transitional demographic profile between young and aging populations.
